records.ety: [Cf. G. centner a hundred-weight, fr. L. centenarius of a hundred, fr. centum a hundred.]

defs: A weight divisible first into a hundred parts, and then into smaller parts.
notes: ☞ The metallurgists use a weight divided into a hundred equal parts, each one pound; the whole they call a centner: the pound is divided into thirty-two parts, or half ounces; the half ounce into two quarters; and each of these into two drams. But the assayers use different weights. With them a centner is one dram, to which the other parts are proportioned.

defs: The commercial hundredweight in several of the continental countries, varying in different places from 100 to about 112 pounds.
